headlines.title: Trump Tells Israel Not to Strike Iran, Saying He 'Calls the Shots'
headlines.description: Iran and Israel exchanged strikes for the first time since the April ceasefire as tensions rise once again in the Middle East. After Iran’s attack, President Donald Trump said he would ask Israel’s Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu "not to retaliate" and that he will have to accept any deal the U.S. negotiates with Iran, saying, “He won’t have any choice…I call all the shots.” NBC’s Richard Engel reports for TODAY.

headlines.title: The Spurs’ secret weapon this postseason? A former coach offering pearls of wisdom
headlines.description: Gregg Popovich coached San Antonio to five NBA titles before stepping aside last year after he had a stroke. He’s still involved, though, giving advice and guidance behind the scenes.

headlines.title: OpenAI files for IPO as AI investment race intensifies
headlines.description: OpenAI announced it has filed to offer its stock on public markets, just a week after its chief rival, Anthropic, did the same.

headlines.title: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang declines Warren’s request to testify at AI hearing
headlines.description: Sen. Elizabeth Warren had invited Huang to discuss Nvidia’s business in China at a Senate committee meeting about America’s AI industry.

headlines.title: Apple renames Siri as ‘Siri AI’ with new push into artificial intelligence
headlines.description: The overhaul puts Siri into more direct competition with modern AI assistants such as ChatGPT.

headlines.title: Brad Paisley criticizes data center planned next to Nashville Zoo
headlines.description: Brad Paisley urged his followers to sign a petition to stop the construction of a 69,000-square-foot data center that would be 50 yards from the zoo.

headlines.title: Chinese President Xi visits North Korea
headlines.description: North Korea hosted Chinese President Xi Jinping for the first time in seven years as the countries agree to expand cooperation in a range of areas from trade to agriculture. NBC News’ Janis Mackey Frayer reports.

headlines.title: Idris Elba says James Bond rumors not ‘realistic,’ some fans won’t accept ‘Black male’ in role
headlines.description: Idris Elba dismissed talk of his playing James Bond, saying certain audiences wouldn’t accept a Black man in the role and that the character shouldn’t be “woke.”

headlines.title: Spencer Pratt Drops to Third Place in LA Mayor’s Race
headlines.description: Votes are still being counted in the closely-watched primary races in California, including for mayor of Los Angeles. Conservative reality TV star Spencer Pratt has dropped to third place behind current democratic Mayor Karen Bass, who is heading to the runoff, and progressive city councilwoman Nithya Raman. NBC’s Liz Kreutz reports for TODAY.

headlines.title: New Jersey police ramp up security as state prepares for World Cup final
headlines.description: As the World Cups heads to 11 cities across the U.S., states are ramping up security to keep fans safe. New Jersey police take NBC News' Sam Brock inside their command center, where they will monitor stadiums, hotels, and training centers ahead of and during matches.

headlines.title: Trump-backed super PAC to host a $1M-per-person fundraiser the day before the White House UFC fight
headlines.description: MAGA Inc., top Trump-backed super PAC, is set to hold a $1 million-per-person fundraiser the day before the UFC matches on the White House grounds.

headlines.title: Defense rests in Karmelo Anthony murder trial after days of emotional testimony
headlines.description: Closing arguments are expected to start Tuesday as jurors were ordered sequestered in their hotel and instructed to stay off social media.
